name: Ibón de Acherito Loop
name_original: Ibón de Acherito – circular desde la Selva de Oza
description: Hike the moderate 6.8-mile Ibón de Acherito Loop in the Aragonese Pyrenees, featuring a pristine glacial lake and stunning mountain views.
region_intro: Hiking around Fago offers diverse terrain characterized by mountainous landscapes, high-altitude lakes, and ancient geological formations. The region features a mix of challenging ascents and moderate paths, often winding through forested areas and open valleys. Hikers can explore trails that lead to significant natural landmarks and historical sites. The varied topography provides opportunities for different levels of outdoor activity. Best hiking trails around Fago The most popular hiking route is Ibón de Acherito Loop, a 6.7 miles (10.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 26 minutes to complete, circling a scenic mountain lake. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ibon de Acherito y Lac d'Ansabere – circular from Refugio de la Mina – Selva de Oza, a difficult 8.2 miles (13.2 km) path that connects two prominent lakes through forest. Local hikers also love the Dolmen de Aguas Tuertas – Achar of Aguas Tuertas loop from Comunero de Ansó y Hecho, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through a landscape featuring ancient megaliths, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Fago is defined by mountain lakes, dense forests, and historical dolmens. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Fago are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Fago's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Fago?
A: Hiking around Fago offers diverse terrain, primarily characterized by mountainous landscapes, high-altitude lakes, and ancient geological formations. You'll find a mix of challenging ascents and moderate paths, often winding through dense forested areas and open valleys. The region's topography provides varied experiences, from rugged mountain trails to serene lakeside walks.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate hiking options in Fago?
A: Yes, Fago offers a range of trails suitable for different abilities. While there are challenging ascents, you can find moderate loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932364096/ibon-de-acherito-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón de Acherito Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.7 miles (10.9 km) and takes about 4 hours 26 minutes, circling a scenic mountain lake. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1608826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Aguas Tuertas – Achar of Aguas Tuertas loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail through ancient megaliths.

Q: What are some challenging hikes available in Fago?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Fago has several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938303275/ibon-de-acherito-y-lac-d-ansabere-circular-from-refugio-de-la-mina-selva-de-oza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibon de Acherito y Lac d'Ansabere – circular from Refugio de la Mina – Selva de Oza&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 8.2 miles (13.2 km) path connecting two prominent lakes through forest. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10527032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Achar of Aguas Tuertas – Dolmen de Aguas Tuertas loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 17.6 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the trails in Fago?
A: Fago's trails lead to several notable natural features and historical landmarks. You can explore stunning high-altitude lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5017237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón de Acherito&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5586805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estanés Lake&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2691256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Ansabère&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to ancient megaliths, like those found along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1608826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Aguas Tuertas&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Fago?
A: Yes, Fago offers several excellent circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932364096/ibon-de-acherito-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón de Acherito Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail around a mountain lake. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1608826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Aguas Tuertas – Achar of Aguas Tuertas loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through a landscape featuring ancient megaliths.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Fago?
A: The routes in Fago are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's stunning mountain lakes, dense forests, and the unique experience of encountering historical dolmens.

Q: Are there any huts or refuges available for hikers in Fago?
A: Yes, for those looking for amenities or overnight stays, there are several huts and refuges in the Fago area. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3141925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ansabère Huts&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4414444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lizara Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4414417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gabardito Refuge&lt;/a&gt;. These can provide a welcome break or a place to rest during longer treks.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Fago?
A: Given Fago's mountainous terrain and high-altitude lakes, the best time for hiking is typically from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and trails are clear of snow, offering optimal conditions for exploration. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Fago?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes available in Fago, ranging from moderate to difficult. This variety ensures options for different skill levels and preferences, allowing you to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options in Fago?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer adventures, Fago offers more extensive routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39433796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Aguas Tuertas – Achar of Aguas Tuertas loop from GR11 & GR65.3.3&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 27.6 km trail, providing a significant challenge and a deeper immersion into the region's landscapes.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Fago?
A: While specific wildlife sightings can vary, mountainous regions like Fago are often home to diverse fauna. Hikers might spot various bird species, including raptors, and potentially encounter mountain goats or deer in quieter areas. Always observe wildlife from a respectful distance.
tips:
This lake, at an altitude of 1,875 m, is the westernmost in the Pyrenees. It is home to a colony of Pyrenean newts with some endemic characteristics.

All the Aragonese lakes are protected by being included in the Inventory of Singular Wetlands, therefore any activity that could modify the quality of their waters is prohibited. While bathing is not specifically mentioned, it is understood that bathing with chemicals on the skin such as sunscreen, deodorant, perfume, or lotion is negatively affecting water quality, so it cannot be done. You can read the protection decree and the list of wetlands included here: http://www.boa.aragon.es/cgi-bin/EBOA/BRSCGI?CMD=VEROBJ&MLKOB=558856590404
During the summer months these types of cabins are reserved exclusively for shepherds. The rest of the year some can serve as improvised shelter if it gets late or the weather gets complicated.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Ibón de Acherito Loop?
faqs.answer: The Ibón de Acherito Loop is a moderate to hard hike that generally takes about 4 to 5 hours to complete. This estimate is for a circular route covering approximately 11-11.5 km with an elevation gain of around 650-750 meters.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike the Ibón de Acherito Loop is typically during summer and on weekends, when it is most popular. While not technically difficult in favorable conditions, be aware that in winter or with hard snow, crampons might be necessary for certain sections. Always check local weather and trail conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the Selva de Oza area. While the exact trailhead can vary slightly depending on the specific variant of the loop, it often begins by ascending the Foyas ravine. Parking is generally available in the vicinity of Selva de Oza, but it can get busy, especially during peak season.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Ibón de Acherito Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and can be challenging. You'll encounter established paths through dense fir and beech forests, as well as more open, rocky sections as you gain elevation. The ascent often follows the Foyas ravine, and the descent usually follows the Acherito ravine. Be prepared for considerable gradients and some paths that run close to precipices, requiring sure-footedness and sturdy footwear.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The highlight of the route is undoubtedly the Ibón de Acherito itself, a pristine glacial lake nestled in a beautiful cirque. You'll also enjoy panoramic views of the Guarrinza valley, the Selva de Oza forests, and various peaks like Peña Forca and Castillo de Acher. Other notable features include the free refuge of Barcal de Acherito, a nearby dolmen, and the Shepherds' Cabin in Parque Natural de los Valles Occidentales.

faqs.question: Is the Ibón de Acherito Loop suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Parque Natural de los Valles Occidentales, it's generally advisable to check specific regulations regarding dogs. While many natural parks in Spain allow dogs, they often require them to be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always respect local signage and guidelines.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Parque Natural de los Valles Occidentales?
faqs.answer: Generally, there is no entrance fee to access the Parque Natural de los Valles Occidentales for hiking. However, specific activities or access to certain areas might require permits or have regulations. For hiking the Ibón de Acherito Loop, a specific permit is not typically required, but it's always good practice to check the official park website for any updated rules or restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Valles Occidentales Natural Park is rich in biodiversity. While hiking, you might be lucky enough to spot chamois (sarrios) on the mountain slopes. The Ibón de Acherito itself is home to a unique colony of Pyrenean newts with endemic characteristics.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route of the Ibón de Acherito Loop intersects with several other paths. Notably, the entire route covers the Camino del Ibón de Acherito. It also passes through sections of the Étape 09, Senda de Camille 7 (Linza-Oza), and the Viejo Camino de Santiago por el Valle de Hecho - Calzada del Summo Pyreneo.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate to hard difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, and appropriate clothing layers for changing mountain weather. A map and compass or a GPS device are also recommended. If hiking in winter or with snow, crampons might be necessary. Always be prepared for steep sections and paths near precipices.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for this loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, while not always technically difficult, some parts of the trail can be challenging. There are sections with considerable gradients and paths that run close to precipices, requiring good physical fitness and sure-footedness. In winter or with hard snow, conditions can become hazardous, potentially requiring specialist equipment and prior alpine experience. Always assess conditions and your own abilities before setting out.

waypoints.name: Shepherds' Cabin in Parque Natural de los Valles Occidentales
